call_params.prompt_last_msg_preview:
{'role': 'user', 'content': '
## Review Form
Below is a description of the questions you will be asked on the review form for each paper and some guidelines on what to consider when answering these questions.
When writing your review, please keep in mind that after decisions have been made, reviews and meta-reviews of accepted papers and opted-in rejected papers will be made public.

1. Summary: Briefly summarize the paper and its contributions. This is not the place to critique the paper;

result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
The paper tackles a relevant and increasingly critical issue: the hidden computational and stability costs of incremental architectural modifications in attention-based models. However, the execution falls short of the rigorous empirical and mechanistic standards required for a top-tier venue. First, the claims regarding a "highly coupled optimization landscape" and "gradient amplification" are purely qualitative. There is no projection-based diagnostic validation (e.g., SVD decay of activation covariances, Fisher discriminant ratios, Hessian/Fisher condition numbers, or stability radii) to substantiate the asserted fragility or quantify representation geometry. Second, the system-scale evaluation is superficial. While peak memory and average epoch time are reported, the study omits first-class latency benchmarks, explicit perturbation-cost accounting, memory-bandwidth utilization profiling, and inference scheduling overhead. Throughput is only measured at a single batch size,
